en.cutsceneStart1: Hey! You must be the newbie!
en.cutsceneStart3: Don’t be! I will explain everything!
en.cutsceneStart4: This is the Wood machine! It will provide wood to build bridges.
en.cutsceneStart5: But it never provides the right amount...
en.cutsceneStart6: Use these transporters to get the exact amount to build the bridge!
en.cutsceneStart7: No more. No less. Got it?
en.cutsceneFinal2: Now let’s pack up. The work here is done!
en.159a105c: Did you know? When we multiply in the base 10 system, each zero in the number will represent an extra zero to the immediate right in the result.
en.68905f75: Did you know? When we multiply in the base 10 system, each zero in the number will represent an extra zero to the immediate right in the result.
en.5afcba32: We already know what happens when we multiply numbers in the base of 10 system. Let's give this puzzle a try!
en.8e3fd009:
Since multiplying makes digits move to the right, dividing makes them move to the left, making them smaller. To understand how much it will move the same trick applies.

Example: 100 moves 2; 10 moves 1; 1,000 moves 3; and so.
en.020b33d6: Ugh, it gave me too much this time…
en.aacd263b:
Decimals have their dots in the middle of them see? 
Multiplying by 10 moves the decimal point to the right and dividing by 10 moves the decimal point to the left. 

The higher the multiple of 10, the more it moves.
en.3d8b2bf3: Look! Decimals! Let’s see how they react to multiplying and dividing.
en.b6cf2b40: What can I do with 0.5 of a plank? I will need to multiply this…
en.250bdffe: The same numbers can be represented in different ways! 50 can be 5 x 10 and also 500 ÷ 10. Let’s see how that works?
en.c1ffc3c9: The solution is coming out different now. Let’s figure it out.
en.52271a97: Did you know the placing in a numbers have names? Let’s check it out.
en.c01d1a48: Nice! Now you know what the placement means and how to say it’s name.
en.8648b220: Now let’s see if we remember those names.
en.175ee669: Last one for the day! I will nail this!
en.ce3bc762: Let's connect the number that represents the names in the number above.
en.4f2b6d7b: Did you know? Each place of digit in every number has a value. This value will increase or decrease depending on the position.
en.a165af7d-45ce-4acb-90e2-2abfacc9eaf6:
Multiplying numbers by multiples of 10, moves their digits to the right, making them bigger.  To understand how much it will move, you can count the quantity of zeros in the multiple of 10. 

Example: 100 moves 2; 10 moves 1; 1,000 moves 3; and so.

Example: 100 moves 2; 10 moves 1; 1,000 moves 3; and so.
